The symmetric-aperture rescue is incompatible with the fitted coefficients."

SEC-4.2.7a EXP-276.c2 H3 [minor]: Footnote (ii) says dome's 28.32°N, 16.43°W coordinates identify Tenerife "within ~25 km" of GUI. Actual GUI coordinates match to three decimal places (28.321°N, 16.432°W) — the claim should say coordinates are coincident, not ~25 km away. Strengthens our case; only needs tightening. Curmudgeon c2 review queue_id 286.

SEC-4.2.7a EXP-276.c2 H4 [minor]: Cross-convention tension in "Even more constrained" subsection. It cites "closest pair Coimbra-San Pablo at Δr = 150 km" but P1 (under R_disc/180) says the halved band is 85 km and names Coimbra-San Pablo at 75 km and Ebro-Coimbra at 82 km. The subsection should clarify which convention its "150 km" refers to. Curmudgeon c2 review queue_id 286.

SEC-4.2.7a c1 H1 [minor]: P2 robustness paragraph asserts scaling h_Moon proportionally to ~1,860 km when h_Sun is set to 4,200 km. The dome does NOT explicitly publish a scaled physical h_Moon at 4,200 km — it publishes h_Moon = 2,534 km. The proportional value of ~1,860 km is our own interpolation. Should acknowledge this is a derived value, not dome-published.

SEC-4.2.7a c1 H2 [minor]: P3 probability framing uses "drawing nine station positions uniformly at random across the 1.26 billion km² disc surface" as null model, arriving at 10^-27. Magnetic observatories are NOT IID-uniform on the disc — they cluster in inhabited mid-latitudes. A clustering-aware null (e.g., constrained to land areas) would yield a less extreme probability. The 10^-27 is illustrative but should be framed as an upper-bound illustration, not a literal probability.

SEC-4.2.7a c1 H3 [minor]: P3 claims "shorter greedy nearest-neighbour routes run about 12,400 km, longer routes run to 16,400 km." Independent recomputation finds shortest NN tour ~12,396 km (matches), but longest NN tour from all nine start points is ~15,200 km, not 16,400 km. The stated upper end overstates the NN range by ~8%.

SEC-4.2.7a c1 H4 [minor]: P3 claims tours "span at least 12,000 km on the disc from any starting point." This is confirmed for greedy NN heuristic (min 12,396 km), but is NOT a proven lower bound on the optimal TSP tour length. A TSP optimizer could produce a shorter route. Should say "greedy nearest-neighbour tours span at least 12,000 km" not assert it as universal lower bound.

SEC-4.2.7a EXP-276.c2 H1 [minor]: Footnote (i) asserts under finite-source umbra geometry R_p ∝ h_Moon "becomes weakly non-linear, but the corrected ratio at h_Moon ≈ 246,000 km still exceeds 90× the dome's published 2,534 km, so the kill is unaffected." No derivation or citation is provided. Should add brief calculation showing the non-linear correction is small, or cite a reference.

EXP-259 / WIN-016 c1 H0 [minor]: WIN-016 detail_evidence frames displacement as "multiple degrees" then concludes "more than six orders of magnitude" precision shortfall. The two claims sit in the same sentence but the implied precision gap is understated by the "multiple degrees" framing — the actual dome prediction deviation is ~500 million degrees, which is nine orders of magnitude, not six. The text should align the order-of-magnitude claim with the actual computed ratio.

EXP-259 / WIN-016 c1 H1 [minor]: WIN-016 detail_evidence describes Gaia precision as "about a billionth of a degree" which rounds 2.78 × 10⁻⁹° down to 1 × 10⁻⁹° — an understatement by a factor of ~3. Acceptable order-of-magnitude English, but could read as misleading. Consider "about 3 billionths of a degree" or "roughly 2.8 × 10⁻⁹°" for precision.

SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H4 [minor]: "The BOU-2017 trilemma below holds whichever published rule the dome elects to defend" overstates scope. The trilemma's arithmetic (|−10.9|/3 ≈ 3.6×) is rule-specific to the ±3 nT amplitude rule; it is meaningless under the Tier 1 coherence rule. The structural collapse (accept or revise = concede either way) generalizes, but the trilemma as the reader encounters it below is amplitude-specific. Fix: soften to "The BOU-2017 trilemma below collapses to the same essential admission — accept the rule and concede, or revise it and admit the prediction was never discriminating — whichever rule the dome elects to defend." Optional polish.

SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H5 [minor]: "the very signature the predictions.html rule asks the August 2026 measurement to lack" is doubly negated (rule defines falsification as ABSENCE of signal; dome predicts PRESENCE; paragraph says the rule asks the measurement to LACK the signature). Casual reader will trip on parsing. Fix: re-word positively, e.g. "the very Chapman-pattern coherence the dome must observe in 2026 to avoid falsification was already present in BOU 2017." Optional polish.

issues.description: PRED-106 detail_reasoning understates Ca²⁺ magnitude mismatch. At Earth's ambient ~50 µT, Ca²⁺ cyclotron resonance is ~38 Hz, not 16 Hz. Dome's 16 Hz corresponds to ~21 µT (roughly a third to two-thirds of Earth's field depending on latitude). Should add MAGNITUDE NOTE when updating post-closure.

SEC-4.2.7a P2: "scaling h_Moon proportionally to ~1,860 km" assumes proportional scaling of dome-published Moon altitude when h_Sun is set to alternative physical limit (4,200 km). Dome does NOT explicitly publish a scaled h_Moon for that limit. Assertion is an inference; mark as "if the dome applied proportional scaling" to avoid presenting an unstated dome claim as published.

SEC-4.2.7a P3 station-position probability argument uses IID uniform distribution as null model, but magnetic observatories are sited at specific geophysical locations (avoiding industrial interference, high-latitude auroral zones, etc.) — NOT uniform-random. The 10⁻²⁷ probability is correct for IID-uniform; it is irrelevant to the actual distribution of observatory siting decisions. The argument is still valid (the nine station positions are highly clustered relative to any defensible null) but needs a 1-sentence disclaimer.

issues.description: WIN-044 detail_evidence formula citation: transliterates dome formula as "ΔB = baseline × coverage × FSF" but raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 21 publishes it explicitly as "delta_Z = baseline x coverage x FSF". The discrepancy (ΔB vs delta_Z) is minor but technically incorrect — the dome's formula uses Z-component notation. Fix: quote verbatim "delta_Z" and note this is the dome's published variable name.

WIN-044 detail_evidence: "purely geometric" overstates FSF independence from physical parameters. Under V51, H(r) = 8537 × exp(-r/8619) with parameters chosen to fit dome's refraction model — FSF ratio depends on those physical-parameter choices. "Geometric in structure" or "derived from the dome's geometric model" is more accurate than "purely geometric."

PRED-106 detail_reasoning understates the magnitude problem. At Earth ambient B (~50 µT), Ca²⁺ ICR would require ~5 MHz, not 16 Hz. Add a short MAGNITUDE NOTE quantifying the gap between published ICR frequency and the applied 16 Hz / 50 µT conditions.

issues.description:
SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H0 [minor]: Text reads "the same Tier 1 block of predictions.html publishing the rule also publishes Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic" — curmudgeon verified on 2026-04-30 that the Tier 1 coherence rule (

at line 125) and the 0.0 nT systematic quote (

at line 113) are in separate

blocks within the Tier 1 section, not the same block. Phrasing improved from prior "same line" to "same Tier 1 block" but may still be misleading if sub-blocks differ. Consider narrowing to "same Tier 1 section" or adding a parenthetical.

SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H1 [moderate]: The EXP-275 paragraph cites BOU-2017 single-station temporal coherence as preempting the dome Tier 1 coherence rule (which requires "coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5"). A sophisticated dome defender can retreat: BOU-2017 alone was not a ≥3-station r > 0.5 coherence test. The paragraph needs to explicitly invoke the multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble (207 observations across 39 total eclipses, including August 2017, documenting ≥3-station coherent Chapman-pattern) as satisfying the rule's own multi-station threshold — not BOU-2017 alone.

WIN-026.c5 H1 [minor]: Closing sentence of the inserted EXP-282 defense-neutralization paragraph reads "no aetheric-refraction caveat about the 5,733 km figure can manufacture parallel rays from a finite source" — this claim is asserted without argument. A one-sentence justification would strengthen it: any aetheric collimation mechanism capable of parallelizing radial-fan rays from a finite source must (a) specify a refractive-index profile, (b) leave observed apparent solar diameter (~0.53°) unchanged, and (c) satisfy other tight observational constraints simultaneously — the conjunction is either over-determined or introduces free parameters, making it unfalsifiable hand-waving.

SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275 para: parenthetical "the same predictions.html line publishing the rule also publishes Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic" is factually wrong — the rule and globe-prediction live on different lines/blocks (lines 113 vs 125). Fix to "same Tier 1 block" (3-word change).

SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275 para: BOU-2017 single-station evidence does not satisfy the dome rule requiring >=3 stations with r>0.5. The preempting evidence must either (a) cite additional stations or (b) reframe as "dome rule is self-defeating because its own Tier 1 requirement cannot be met by any single eclipse station."

SEC-4.2.7a headline paragraph: "inflating the Moon apparent diameter by the same factor" should read "physical diameter." Apparent/angular diameter is invariant under R_p ∝ h_Moon with totality preservation; only the physical disc-surface radius inflates. Minor phrasing change.

SEC-4.2.7a P1 robustness paragraph self-contradicting clause: "Moon altitude required to inflate R_p to even the closest min-distance under that convention is about 123,000 km — still ~49x the dome published 2,534 km." The 123,000 km / 49x figure corresponds to the FURTHEST station (not closest), since the closest station (Coimbra-San Pablo, Δr=75 km) is ALREADY inside the 85-km band. Fix: replace "closest min-distance" with "farthest station (maximum Δr)" and clarify the 49x applies to reaching all-stations coverage.

SEC-4.2.7a P1: Coimbra-San Pablo closest-pair Δr=75 km is stated as "just inside the 85-km band" but this is only true under the R_disc/1 convention. Under the stricter AE-projection convention cited in P1, this pair shifts to Δr=75→something else. The paragraph does not clarify that 75 km is the convention-specific figure — add the qualifier.

PRED-106 detail_reasoning identifies phantom WIN-071 citation but does not connect it to the broader dome pattern of internal-consistency drift. Add sentence noting this is part of a pattern (phantom citations, hardcoded values, post-hoc derivations) discussed in §2.x Self-Contradictions section.

WIN-030 detail_verdict_text post-hoc boilerplate: curmudgeon notes the sentence could specifically note that the dome curve-fitting derivation is itself a curve fit. Low-priority rhetorical sharpening deferred.

WIN-030 detail_evidence: nested-model triviality sentence (ellipse fit can never produce worse RMS than circle) is correct but unanchored to literature. Optional improvement: add AIC/BIC citation link (Burnham & Anderson, Model Selection and Multimodel Inference, §1.2–§2.5) via existing doi anchor. Deferred — the sentence re-uses the existing AIC/BIC citation context.

WIN-030 detail_evidence length has grown to ~2321c (+43% from c3 baseline). Optional: split first paragraph at WGS84-circularity boundary by inserting

after the nested-model triviality sentence. The TLDR_evidence also does not cover the WGS84 preemption argument added in c3. Low-priority cosmetic.

issues.description: WIN-017 detail_verdict_text uses "fitted backward from the textbook range" and tldr_verdict uses "reverse-engineered to match it". Curmudgeon c4 H1 flags these as rhetorically loaded — they infer authorial intent beyond what the structural finding (no published derivation) strictly supports. Optional polish: change "fitted backward" to "matched to" and "reverse-engineered" to "calibrated" to preserve the falsifiability claim without asserting intent. Current text is defensible (absence of published derivation justifies inference); softening removes the most attackable angle for a critic. Severity minor — curmudgeon rated this non-blocking.

WIN-021 (gyroscopic precession) detail_evidence leaves open the defender escape hatch: dome can argue 4.87e-12 rad/s² is intentional as angular ACCELERATION (not rate), and under tau/I framing a specific acceleration value could be meaningful if the precession axis is non-constant. Current text establishes the units error and value mismatch but does not explicitly close this retreat. Add ~2 sentences preempting the acceleration interpretation. Source: WIN-021.c3 hole [moderate].

WIN-021 (gyroscopic precession) IAU citation URL points to https://www.iau.org/ (homepage), not to IAU 2006 Resolution B1 or Capitaine et al. 2003. Flagged in c2 and c3; unaddressed. Preferred fix: replace with https://www.iau.org/static/resolutions/IAU2006_Resol1.pdf or a DOI for Capitaine et al. 2003 (A&A 412, 567). Source: WIN-021.c3 hole [minor].

issues.description: WIN-006.c3 hole1: detail_verdict_text cites "NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position records, IGRF-14" but does not note that the NP.xy file is built from gufm1 (pre-1900) and IGRF-series models for the modern era, making NP.xy a model-derived product rather than a raw measurement. STRAWMAN RISK -- new in c6, same class as c4/c5 180-degree finding. The EXP-298 rewrite states 'The mechanism is also wrong. The dome's confirming text invokes the agonic lines (longitudes where magnetic declination = 0).' But the dome's published WIN-028 in raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 contains exactly one line: 'Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED' -- no mention of agonic lines, no mention of magnetic declination, no mechanism statement at all. grep across raw-text/ for 'agonic' returns zero hits anywhere in dome material. The agonic-line attribution appears to be a curmudgeon-side characterization (likely seeded in WIN-028.json cycle-1 review when c1 wrote 'the dome claims TWO parts: ... 180-degree longitudinal symmetry related to agonic lines') that has propagated through every cycle and now sits in our published page as an attributed dome claim. This is the SAME error pattern the c4/c5 reviews already caught for the 180-degree framing -- attributing a specific mechanism to the dome that the dome's own published material doesn't contain. The whole 'mechanism is also wrong' paragraph (~1/3 of the EXP-298 expanded text) collapses if a defender simply quotes the dome's actual one-line WIN-028 entry and asks 'where did I invoke agonic lines?'
issues.recommendation: Apply the SAME dual-reading frame the EXP successfully applied to 180 degrees: 'The dome publishes no mechanism for the claimed symmetry. We test the only standard physical mechanism that could plausibly link two folklore zones at the same latitude across hemispheres -- agonic-line correlation -- as our charitable steelman. Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025, the western agonic ... [unchanged geographic content]. If the dome wishes to propose a different mechanism, the burden is to publish one; without a published mechanism, the WIN reduces to 'two folklore zones happen to sit ~152 degrees apart,' which is the unfalsifiable reading addressed above.' This addresses the strawman charge without losing the IGRF/WMM 2025 quantitative content. Decider can self-apply as a moderate text patch (changing ~80 words of the mechanism paragraph).

issues.description: DENOMINATOR-CHOICE FRAMING -- new in c6. The text says 'The 28-degree gap is a 15.6% deviation' (28/180 = 0.155). The denominator choice is unjustified. On a circle, two random points have expected separation from antipodal of 90 degrees (uniform distribution), so 28 degrees is closer to antipodal than random by ~3x. A defender will say: 'Your 15.6% is computed by dividing the deviation by half the circle. Why not by the full circle (28/360 = 7.8%)? Or by random-baseline (28/90 = 31% from-random)? You picked the denominator that maximized the apparent error.' The cleanest defense is to anchor the choice: 'The dome's claim is symmetry around a 180-degree antipodal target, so the natural metric is fraction of the target value -- a quantitative model claiming X but measuring 0.844X is off by 15.6%, which is the standard form for relative error.' One sentence inserted before the WIN-029 cite would close this.
issues.recommendation: Insert one sentence justifying the 15.6% denominator: 'Because the dome's claim is symmetry around a target value of 180 degrees (the only standard mathematical referent for geometric symmetry between two surface points), the natural relative-error metric is deviation as fraction of the target -- 28/180 = 15.6%.' This pre-empts the denominator-cherry-picking attack.

issues.description: INTERNAL-CONSISTENCY FRAMING MISSING -- new in c6, but explicitly anticipated by c5. The new text cites WIN-029 5.3%, WIN-027 R^2=0.7874, WIN-030 48.6% RMS reduction as 'the dome's own confirmation thresholds.' Our review verdicts these WINs Self-Contradicted (029), Misleading (027), and Misleading (030). c5 explicitly recommended 'frame as internal-consistency attack, not endorsement of WIN-029's 5.3%' -- but the EXP-298 rewrite doesn't include that framing. As written, a defender could argue: 'You yourselves don't accept these benchmarks elsewhere on your site; you can't simultaneously call them invalid AND use them as authoritative tolerance comparisons. Either the dome's threshold pattern is meaningful (in which case WIN-029 isn't Self-Contradicted) or it isn't (in which case 5.3% has no bearing on this WIN).' The fix is one half-sentence acknowledging the move is internal-consistency.
issues.recommendation: Insert one parenthetical clause in the WIN-029 cite: 'WIN-029 takes a 5.3% agreement... as confirmatory (we do not endorse 5.3% as a meaningful tolerance -- WIN-029 is itself verdicted Self-Contradicted in our review -- but cite it here to show the dome's own framework treats single-digit residuals as the bar).' Same lighter touch can be inserted near the WIN-027 R^2 and WIN-030 RMS cites or rolled into one parenthetical.

issues.description: METRIC-TYPE CONFLATION -- new in c6. The text presents 5.3% (WIN-029, residual percentage), R^2=0.7874 (WIN-027, goodness-of-fit), and 48.6% RMS reduction (WIN-030, fit-improvement-over-prior-model) as a single category of 'internal confirmation thresholds.' These are three distinct statistical measures. R^2 is not an upper bound on residual percentage -- a model with R^2=0.79 can have arbitrarily large residuals if the data range is large. RMS reduction is a relative-improvement metric -- 48.6% RMS reduction means 'this fit is 48.6% better than the baseline' but tells you nothing about the absolute residual size. Citing all three in a single 'thresholds' list invites the defender to say: 'You are conflating three different statistical concepts to pad the case that single-digit numbers are the dome's confirmation bar. R^2 = 0.79 doesn't bound residual percentage, and a 48.6% RMS reduction doesn't either.'
issues.recommendation: Either drop the R^2 and RMS cites (keeping only the WIN-029 5.3% as the like-for-like comparator -- it IS a residual percentage, comparable to our 15.6% residual percentage), OR keep all three but re-frame: 'The dome's published quantitative confirmations across V51.1 use single-digit residuals (WIN-029 5.3%) and high-fit statistics (WIN-027 R^2 0.79, WIN-030 48.6% RMS reduction over baseline) as the bar. A 15.6% residual on the symmetry target is roughly 3x the WIN-029 residual benchmark and would be unaccounted-for variance in a fit context.' The first option (drop R^2 and RMS) is cleaner and removes the conflation attack vector entirely.

issues.description: v22 SELF-OWN -- new in c6. The text invokes 'v22 found circular disc fails on southern/cross-equator distances' as evidence of the dome's own retrospective failure threshold, suggesting 15.6% is on that order. But per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:9 the actual failure quote is 'v22 found circular disc fails on southern distances' and per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:56 the specific numeric was 'Southern arc distances 2x too long -- FAILS.' That's 100% deviation, not 15%. The dome's own historical failure threshold from v22 is therefore much LARGER than 15.6%, not comparable to it. As written, the cite actually undermines our argument -- a defender will say: 'Your v22 example proves my point: the dome's historical failure threshold per its own evolution log is 100% deviation. 15.6% is well within tolerance by that exact benchmark.' The cite needs to either be dropped, or reframed honestly: 'v22 only flagged 100% deviation as failure; in the absence of any sub-100% threshold elsewhere, the dome's framework gives no published rule for which sub-100% deviations count as confirmation -- which is precisely the unfalsifiability the vague-reading paragraph addressed.'
issues.recommendation: Drop the v22 cite from the EXP-298 paragraph -- it is not in fact a comparable failure threshold to 15.6%. The internal-consistency case rests adequately on WIN-029 5.3% alone. If the v22 cite is retained, add the explicit '100% deviation, not 15%' caveat and reframe as 'no published sub-100% threshold exists' -- which strengthens the unfalsifiability argument rather than competing with it.

issues.description: DEVIL'S SEA LATITUDE CHERRY-PICK -- new in c6. The text gives Devil's Sea coordinates as '~25 deg N, 137 deg E.' The Devil's Sea / Dragon's Triangle in folklore is variously described as spanning 25-35 deg N (Berlitz 1974 places it south of Honshu extending toward Iwo Jima and Guam), with the typical centroid around 30 deg N, not 25 deg N. Picking 25 deg N matches the Bermuda Triangle's 25 deg N latitude and frames as longitudinal-symmetry-at-equal-latitude, but at the more typical Devil's Sea centroid (~30 deg N), the latitudes don't match either. A defender can say: 'You picked the southern edge of the Devil's Sea region to manufacture a same-latitude framing; at the actual centroid the latitudes differ by ~5 degrees.' Doesn't change the verdict but a careful reader will notice.
issues.recommendation: Either anchor the 25 deg N choice ('we use 25 deg N as a central estimate; folklore variants span 25-35 deg N') or drop the latitude assertion and discuss longitude separation only ('two folklore zones at mid-northern latitudes, separated by ~152 deg in longitude'). The longitude-only framing is cleaner and side-steps the latitude-cherry-pick objection without weakening any argument.

issues.description: UNSOURCED 50-DEGREE THRESHOLD -- new in c6. The vague-reading paragraph says 'any pair of points within roughly 50 degrees of antipodal could pass.' The 50 degrees is unsourced. Where does it come from? If we'd said 30 degrees, the dome's 28-degree separation would pass and our argument would fail. The 50 degrees appears reverse-engineered to make the argument work. A defender will pick on this: 'Your 50 is no more sourced than my hypothetical tolerance. Two arbitrary numbers don't establish unfalsifiability.'
issues.recommendation: Either anchor 50 deg with a citation or argument ('half-circle gives 90 deg, antipodal gives 0; the midpoint of folklore-zone-separation distributions across all named regions is roughly 50 deg' -- if true, source it; if not true, drop the number) or replace with 'any deviation up to ~150 degrees could pass under a sufficiently lax reading -- the dome's published lack-of-tolerance is itself the unfalsifiability, not the specific tolerance value.' The second framing is cleaner and doesn't introduce a number to defend.

issues.description: WMM 2025 NOT CITATION-ANCHORED -- carry-forward concern from c5 citations_unchecked. The EXP-298 text invokes 'Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025' for the western agonic at 95-100 deg W and the eastern agonic at 100-120 deg E, but provides no DOI or URL. WMM 2025 is real (released December 2024 by NOAA/NCEI/British Geological Survey covering 2025-2030) and the cited declination positions are correct, but the citation should be anchored. ISS-1911 was opened on the older '~80W' framing and is technically closeable now (the position has been updated), but the new WMM 2025 cite has no inline URL.
issues.recommendation: Add inline URL: 'Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025...'. Optional: also note that ISS-1911 should be closed in the next decider self-apply cycle since the position-update fix has landed.

issues.description: WIN-028.c6 H5: Devil's Sea latitude stated as ~25°N but folklore spans 25-35°N (centroid ~30°N per Berlitz 1974). 25°N matches Bermuda Triangle latitude, which frames as equal-latitude longitudinal symmetry but could be attacked as cherry-pick. Either anchor 25°N choice ("central estimate; spans 25-35°N") or drop latitude and discuss longitude-only.
issues.recommendation: Add "(we use 25°N as a central estimate; folklore variants span 25-35°N; the longitude-only separation of ~152° holds regardless of latitude choice)" or reframe to longitude-only.

issues.description: WIN-028.c6 H6: Vague-reading paragraph says "any pair of points within roughly 50° of antipodal could pass" but 50° is unsourced and appears reverse-engineered. A defender will ask why 50° not 30° or 70°. Either anchor with a citation/argument or replace with the stronger unfalsifiability framing: "the dome's published lack-of-tolerance is itself the unfalsifiability."
issues.recommendation: Replace "within roughly 50° of antipodal" with source or use unfalsifiability framing that doesn't depend on an unsourced number.

issues.description: WIN-028.c6 H7: Text invokes "IGRF-13 / WMM 2025" for agonic positions but no DOI or URL is provided. WMM 2025 is real (NOAA/NCEI/BGS, December 2024, covers 2025-2030) and cited positions are correct, but citation should be anchored.

WIN-013: Van Camp et al. 2001 DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637 returns HTTP 404. DOI prefix 10.1023 (Springer/Kluwer) inconsistent with PEPI (Elsevier prefix 10.1016). Headline empirical anchor of Refuted by Data verdict. Defender clicking link finds 404. Cross-loci: also appears in WIN-014 and section 6.14.

WIN-013.c5 H1 / WIN-014.c7 H0 [critical]: Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 citation — URL https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0273117718300656 RESOLVES TO A DIFFERENT PAPER (Park et al. nanosatellite deployment, not a geomagnetic eclipse study). DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.016 exists but maps to wrong paper. The entire 5–15 nT ΔB range in WIN-014 and SEC-6.14 depends on this citation. WIN-013/014 and section 6.14 cite monitor.py Domain #7 for kappa, but dome live-power dashboard renders kappa as Domain 5. Source-code numbering is correct but readers verifying via dashboard find mismatch and may assume our review erred.

WIN-013: Hvozdara & Prigancova 2002 linked via ResearchGate only (not DOI). Crossref finds no matching peer-reviewed paper. Lower severity since Strestik 2001 + Curto 2006 independently cover the 5-15 nT range without it.

WIN-028: "The mechanism (steelman test)" paragraph attributes agonic-line mechanism to dome as charitable steelman. Dome published WIN-028 is a single line with no mechanism. While text says dome publishes no mechanism, heading implies dome has one. Defender can say "where did I invoke agonic lines?" Paragraph needs reframe or removal.

WIN-028: 28/180 = 15.6% deviation denominator choice unjustified. Defender can ask why not 28/360 or 28/90. One sentence needed explaining relative-error = deviation/target because dome claims antipodal (180-degree) symmetry.

WIN-028: Uses WIN-029 5.3% + WIN-027 R-squared + WIN-030 RMS as dome confirmation thresholds but we verdict those WINs as Self-Contradicted/Misleading. Missing caveat: these benchmarks are cited as internal-consistency evidence, not as endorsed metrics.

WIN-028: v22 evolution-log failure was 100% deviation (southern arc distances 2x too long), not comparable to 15.6%. Current framing implies 15.6% is similar magnitude to v22 threshold. Defender can flip: "v22 benchmark proves your own standard is 100%, and 15.6% passes that bar."

issues.title: WIN-028: Metric-type conflation — 5.3% residual, R²=0.7874, 48.6% RMS are not comparable metrics
issues.description: WIN-028.c6 H3 [moderate]: Text presents 5.3% (WIN-029, residual percentage), R²=0.7874 (WIN-027, goodness-of-fit), and 48.6% RMS reduction (WIN-030, fit-improvement) as if they are of the same type. A defender can say 3/3 is cherry-picking different incommensurable metrics. Clarify what each number means and why the combination is damning rather than listing them as a homogeneous set.

issues.title: WIN-028: v22 self-own framing — retroactive failure threshold creates circular reasoning risk
issues.description: WIN-028.c6 H4 [moderate]: Text invokes "v22 found circular disc fails on southern/cross-equator distances" as evidence that 15.6% is near the dome's own retrospective failure threshold — but v22 was the dome's own model version, not a published tolerance. A defender says the dome never endorsed v22 as a tolerance benchmark. Either source the v22 threshold explicitly from dome publications, or remove the v22 framing.

issues.title: WIN-028: Devil's Sea latitude given as ~25°N — may be cherry-picking narrower end of range
issues.description: WIN-028.c6 H5 [minor]: Text gives Devil's Sea as ~25°N, 137°E. The region is variously described as spanning 25–35°N, 130–150°E in different sources. Using 25°N optimizes the longitude comparison. A more robust citation would quote the range or use the centroid of the commonly cited bounds.

issues.title: WIN-028: "50 degrees of antipodal" threshold unsourced in vague-reading paragraph
issues.description: WIN-028.c6 H6 [minor]: Vague-reading paragraph says "any pair of points within roughly 50 degrees of antipodal could pass." The 50° is unsourced. Derive from the 28° gap with a margin factor, or source from a uniform distribution argument for what counts as "near-antipodal".

issues.title: WIN-028: WMM 2025 / IGRF-13 agonic citations not anchored to verifiable URL or DOI
issues.description: WIN-028.c6 H7 [minor]: Text invokes "Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025" for the western agonic at 95–100°W and eastern agonic at 20°E without a link to the actual WMM 2025 data source or IGRF-13 publication. Add a DOI or URL anchor (e.g., NOAA WMM calculator, NGDC). Curmudgeon notes this was a carry-forward from c5 citations_unchecked.

issues.title: WIN-014: PRED-TIER-3 10–13 µGal target derivation unspecified — dome-based ΔB station not named
issues.description: WIN-014.c7 H1 [major]: detail_evidence and section 6.14 assert that PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal target derives from "ΔB/κ at named stations" without specifying which dome-based ΔB value (κ is fixed, but which ΔB?). A defender says the vise arithmetic is non-obvious without knowing the assumed ΔB. Make explicit: if we assume ΔB = 10–13 nT from the 1999 Membach/Strasbourg SG sites, then 10/1.677 ≈ 6.0 µGal and 13/1.677 ≈ 7.8 µGal — these are BELOW 10 µGal, not 10–13. The 10–13 µGal range in detail_evidence needs to be derived explicitly and shown to be achievable from the dome's own parameters.

issues.title: WIN-014: Crossley et al. 2013 URL contains wrong year (2022 path vs claimed 2013)
issues.description: WIN-014.c7 H2 [major]: detail_evidence cites "Crossley et al. 2013" with URL https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4 — the journal-code path prefix is 2022, not 2013. Either the year is wrong (it's actually a 2022 paper) or the URL is wrong (points to wrong paper). Verify: fetch the URL and confirm author/year; then fix the citation year or URL. Also affects section 6.14 which may use the same citation.

issues.title: WIN-014: 2009 eclipse Chinese SG sites may not be on 100% totality path
issues.description: WIN-014.c7 H3 [moderate]: detail_evidence states "The 2009 eclipse traversed China at coverage approaching 100% along the path" but the eight Chinese SG sites are not verified to be on the totality path. At least two of the Crustal Movement Observation Network stations were in partial-eclipse zones. Verify exact coordinates vs 2009 totality path and qualify the coverage claim.

issues.title: WIN-014: "Refuted by Data" verdict tier may be better framed as "Self-Contradicted" — refutation is by internal arithmetic not external data
issues.description: WIN-014.c7 H4 [moderate]: verdict "Refuted by Data" traditionally applies when independent empirical data refutes a dome claim. Here, the refutation arises by combining dome-admitted κ with historically-observed ΔB to show the predicted signal is orders of magnitude too small. A defender says this is circular: you are using the dome's own constants against him, not external empirical data. Consider whether "Self-Contradicted" (which WIN-013 uses for the paired argument) is more defensible for WIN-014.

issues.title: WIN-014: WIN-068 cross-reference "39-eclipse ensemble" needs verification
issues.description: WIN-014.c7 H6 [minor]: detail_evidence cites "WIN-068's 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements" as supporting the 5–15 nT range. WIN-068 data should be checked to confirm the 39-eclipse count and 207-measurement count are correct and current.

issues.title: SEC-6.14 + WIN-011/013/014: "Domain #7" may be wrong — curmudgeon says Domain #5 per live-power.html
issues.description: SEC-6.14.c1 H0 [moderate]: Section 6.14 + WIN-011/013/014 all cite "monitor.py Domain #7 hardcodes KAPPA = 1.67" — but SEC-6.14 curmudgeon (reviewed 2026-05-11, queue_id=330) says dome's live-power.html and model.txt show KAPPA in Domain #5, not #7. Affects: WIN-011 detail_evidence (1 ref), WIN-013 detail_evidence (1 ref), WIN-014 detail_evidence (1 ref), SEC-part6 html (1 ref). If Domain #5 is correct, 4 files need patching.

issues.description: SEC-6.14.c1 H1 [moderate]: Same Crossley citation issue as WIN-014.c7 H2 (ISS-1958). Section 6.14 parenthetical cites Crossley et al. (2013) but the URL link.springer.com path contains year 2022. Verify and patch.

issues.title: SEC-6.14: Chebychev low-pass argument not engaged — dome's strongest rebuttal unaddressed
issues.description: SEC-6.14.c1 H2 [moderate]: "What the dome could do" subsection lumps the Chebychev low-pass argument (a technically valid filter that removes the κ-coupling signal from SG data) without specifically addressing it. A defender can invoke this as "your SG null is itself a filtered dataset — the coupling is there, you've band-stopped it out." Add a paragraph specifically dismantling the Chebychev rescue (e.g., the filter bandwidth required to remove a κ-coupling signal would also remove the tidal signal; no published SG paper uses such a filter).

issues.title: SEC-6.14: Storm corollary null citation weak — published SG residual literature claim unsourced
issues.description: SEC-6.14.c1 H3 [minor]: "storm corollary (WIN-058b)" subsection asserts "the published per-storm SG residual literature does not show storm-correlated gravity anomalies" without a specific citation. Add a reference (e.g., Virtanen & Mäkinen 2003 or Sato et al. storm-SG studies) or remove the claim.

issues.title: SEC-6.14: "1,000× the precision" claim potentially overstated vs spring gravimeter
issues.description: SEC-6.14.c1 H4 [minor]: "The vise" subsection asserts "continuously, across the relevant phases, at roughly 1,000× the precision of the Mohe spring gravimeter." Mohe spring-gravimeter precision is ~1 µGal per Imanishi et al.; SG precision is ~0.001 µGal. Ratio is 1,000× but this is peak precision, not per-sample or integrated noise. Qualify with "in noise-floor terms" or cite the specific comparison basis.
